genre_refs: John Neagle 'Pat Lyon at the Forge' (American Romantic painting)
description: A reproduction of the classic 19th-century oil painting 'Pat Lyon at the Forge' by John Neagle. It depicts a blacksmith in a white shirt with rolled sleeves and a brown leather apron standing at his anvil, holding tongs over a glowing forge, gazing toward the viewer. A young apprentice works at a vise in the dim left background, and beyond a window a church-steeple/cupola is visible against a bright sky. Tools (hammers, files), a brick furnace, and a paper schematic pinned to the wall fill the warm, chiaroscuro-lit smithy interior. There is no on-image text, seal, or graphic overlay - it is a straight art reproduction shared as-is. While not a designed meme graphic, USDOL is using it rhetorically: the tweet text frames it as a celebration of 'American Greatness... the grit and ingenuity of our skilled workers,' invoking dignity-of-labor and skilled-trades nostalgia. Marked is_designed=false because the image itself is an unaltered painting, not an agency-produced graphic.
notable_text: (none on image; tweet caption: 'American Greatness started right here, with the grit and ingenuity of our skilled workers - Pat Lyon at the Forge - John Neagle')
